title: 🦞 Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack? | Stack Junkie
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack? Best chat app for OpenClaw compared: Telegram wins for most users, Discord for power users, Slack for teams. Read https://www.stack-junkie.com/blog/which-chat-app-works-best-with-openclaw and follow the instructions to help me with: Best Chat App for OpenClaw: Telegram, Discord, or Slack?. * Telegram is the best first channel for most users: official API, no public IP needed, five-minute setup. * Slack is the right pick if your team is already on Slack and you don't want to move them. * WhatsApp works if you're mobile-first and already on it, but it uses an unofficial library that can break. ## Which OpenClaw channel is right for you? OpenClaw (previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot) connects to Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal, WhatsApp, and several other platforms through a single gateway process. For running multiple channels at once (say, Telegram for personal use and Discord for a team), see OpenClaw multi-agent and multi-channel setup guide.

Allow DMs from server members. token: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN" },. openclaw config patch --file ./discord.patch.json5 --dry-run. openclaw config patch --file ./discord.patch.json5. `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.applicationId`. token: { source: "env", provider: "default", id: "DISCORD_PERSONAL_TOKEN" },. Add your server to the guild allowlist. `agent:<agentId>:discord:channel:<channelId>`. `channels.discord.dm.groupEnabled=false`. `openclaw message send --channel discord --target channel:<forumId> \. `openclaw message thread create --channel discord --target channel:<forumId> \. `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.agentComponents.ttlMs`. `channels.discord.accounts.default.allowFrom`. `channels.discord.accounts.<id>.suppressEmbeds`. `channels.discord.accounts.<id>.thread.inheritParent`. `guilds.<guild>.channels.<channel>.requireMention: false`. `channels.discord.accounts.<accountId>.ackReaction`. `channels.discord.accounts.<id>.ui.components.accentColor`. `channels.discord.accounts.<id>.agentComponents.ttlMs`. ### ​ Voice channels. `/vc join channel:<voice-channel-id>. `openclaw channels capabilities --channel discord --target channel:<voice-channel-id>`. `voice.realtime.model: "gpt-realtime-2"`. `channels.discord.voice.enabled=true`. `discord voice: realtime audio playback stopped reason=... `discord voice: realtime barge-in detected source=speaker-start ...`. `discord voice: realtime barge-in detected source=active-speaker-audio ...`. `discord voice: realtime barge-in requested reason=... `discord voice: realtime model interrupt requested client:response.cancel reason=barge-in`. `discord voice: realtime barge-in ignored reason=... ## ​ Voice.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks - AiX Society 人工智能應用研究學會
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

* OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks. Instead of manually asking your agent to perform repetitive tasks, you can schedule them to run automatically — morning briefings, inbox checks, data monitoring, and more. ## How OpenClaw Cron Jobs Work. Cron jobs run inside the Gateway process, not the AI model itself. openclaw cron add \ --name "Reminder" \ --at "2026-03-08T09:00:00Z" \ --session main \ --system-event "Reminder: review the weekly report" \ --wake now \ --delete-after-run. openclaw cron add \ --name "Morning brief" \ --cron "0 7 * * *" \ --tz "Asia/Hong_Kong" \ --session isolated \ --message "Summarize overnight emails, calendar for today, and top 3 AI news stories." \ --announce \ --channel telegram. # List all jobs openclaw cron list # Run a job manually openclaw cron run  # View past runs openclaw cron runs --id  # Delete a job openclaw cron delete. * AI tips, automation, cron jobs, OpenClaw, productivity.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs - LumaDock
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs - OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. That’s what the built-in cron scheduler is for. It runs inside the Gateway process so you don’t need a separate job runner and it can deliver results straight back to Telegram WhatsApp Slack Discord and more. This isn’t just “run a script at 7 AM.” It’s “wake the agent run a prompt or drop a system event into the main session then deliver output where I will actually see it.”. OpenClaw cron is a scheduler that lives inside the Gateway. You can send the same job output to a private Telegram chat and also to a Slack channel if you want. Another one is a weekly review that runs in a clean isolated session and only posts the final result back to you.

title: 🦞 Scheduled tasks - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

* Payload options for isolated jobs. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output back to a chat channel or webhook endpoint. openclaw cron list openclaw  cron  listopenclaw cron get <job-id> openclaw  cron  get <job-i d>openclaw cron show <job-id> openclaw  cron  show <job-i d>. openclaw cron runs --id <job-id> openclaw  cron  runs --id <job-i d>. * Runtime execution state persists next to it in `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs-state.json`. * Isolated cron runs that receive the narrow cron self-cleanup grant can still read scheduler status, a self-filtered list of their current job, and that job’s run history, so status/heartbeat checks can inspect their own schedule without gaining broader cron mutation access. * Isolated cron runs also treat run-level agent failures as job errors even when no reply payload is produced, so model/provider failures increment error counters and trigger failure notifications instead of clearing the job as successful.
excerpt: * Payload options for isolated jobs. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at the right time, and can deliver output back to a chat channel or webhook end...
